name: Countdown
description: Counts down to a date and time. Great for launches and events.

name: Before / after
description: An image comparison slider with a draggable divider.

name: Theme toggle
description: A light / dark switch your visitors can click. Remembers their choice and stays in sync with the site.

name: CAI score card
description: The Codebase Assurance Index score card: band chip, band-inked score, the worst→best band ladder with a “you are here” marker, trend sparkline, lens bars and detail rows. Fetches the published gallery (/api/oss) when an API base is set and renders the HERO — the highest-scoring published repo; falls back to the labelled sample.

props.name: api-base
props.label: Live API base URL (empty = show the sample)

props.name: owner
props.label: Owner — pick a real published repo (with name)

props.name: name
props.label: Repo name — pick a real published repo (with owner)

props.name: card
props.label: Sample-card fallback (JSON — name, owner, score, series, arcFirst, arcBest, lenses, rows)

props.name: seal-text
props.label: Seal badge text (e.g. ✓ Signed by Watchdog)

props.name: href
props.label: Link the whole card to a URL

name: CAI score-card gallery
description: A responsive grid of CAI score cards — the public-record home gallery of real published surveys (quiet-peer cards, not the accent hero artifact). Fetches the published gallery (/api/oss) when an API base is set and shows three distinct cards, hero excluded — the second-best by score, the most-improved by delta, and a random one; falls back to the labelled sample cards.

props.name: api-base
props.label: Live API base URL (empty = show the sample)

props.name: cards
props.label: Sample-cards fallback (JSON array of card objects)

name: CAI band scale
description: The canonical CAI presentation scale: five bands worst→best, cutlines 25/50/70/90, the display words, and an optional Score pin. Pins at the HERO published card's bestScore (the highest-scoring published repo, from /api/oss) when an API base is set; falls back to the seeded score.

props.name: api-base
props.label: Live API base URL (empty = use the seeded score)

name: CAI composition bar
description: A pure-SVG proportion bar for the brilliant / fine / slop code-composition split, coloured with the CAI band vocabulary. Builds the segments from the HERO published card's measured brilliant%/slop% (the highest-scoring published repo, from /api/oss) when an API base is set; falls back to the seeded segments.

props.name: api-base
props.label: Live API base URL (empty = show the sample)

props.name: segments
props.label: Sample segments fallback (JSON array of {label, pct, band})

name: CAI evidence flow
description: A left→right node strip with arrow connectors and an optional dashed loop-back path — the produce → hold → consume / measure → fix → prove diagram. Nodes come from a JSON array of { title, body, tone }.

props.name: loop-label
props.label: Loop-back label (draws a dashed return path when set)

name: CAI C4 architecture heat-map
description: The C4 architecture heat-map carousel — bounded contexts and how they talk, coloured by health — the same map watchdog.canine.dev renders in the app's C4 wheel. Live only: fetches the public C4 list (/api/public/c4, richest first) when an API base is set and cycles the repos with prev/next, loading each one's real C4 heat SVG; shows nothing without a live source.

props.name: api-base
props.label: Live API base URL (required — this widget is live-only)

name: CAI findings a scanner can't produce
description: The deterministic architecture / domain-model / event findings located to file:line, from real published reports — a carousel with the honest “showing X of Y” and a link to the full report. Fetches the public findings feed (/api/public/findings, strongest repo first) when an API base is set and cycles the repos with prev/next; falls back to a small labelled sample.

props.name: api-base
props.label: Live API base URL (empty = show the sample)

name: Contact form
description: A contact form with a topic selector (?topic= prefill), name / email / organisation / message fields and a spam honeypot. Submits via fetch() to the configurable `action` endpoint (the imprint editor's anonymous /api/contact) and reports success/failure inline — the inbox address stays server-side, never in the page. A mailto: to the fallback email survives only as the legacy no-action mode.

props.name: fallback-email
props.label: Legacy mailto target (used — and rendered — only when no action is set)

props.name: action
props.label: Form POST endpoint (empty = legacy mailto to the fallback email)
